race.name: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek
race.tagline: Three alpine summits. 235 km. 4,000 meters of climbing. Australia's definitive mountain sportive.

vitals.registration: Online via bicyclenetwork.com.au. Opens mid-year prior. Cost: AUD $200-250 (~$130-165 USD).
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple feed stations at key points — valley floors and summit approaches. Water, electrolytes, food.
vitals.cutoff_time: Strict time limits at checkpoints. Riders failing to meet intermediate cutoffs are pulled from course.
route_options: 3 Peaks Challenge: 235 km (146 mi), 4,000m climbing — the full Triple Peak
Peaks Challenge 7 Peaks: shorter options available on specific peaks

climate.description: March in the Victorian Alps means warm-to-hot valley floors (28-35C / 82-95F) and significantly cooler summits (15-22C / 59-72F). The event starts before dawn to maximize cool riding time and beat the afternoon heat in the valleys. Bushfire smoke has been a factor in some years. Generally dry, but alpine thunderstorms are possible in the afternoon.
challenges: Extreme valley heat: 30-35C on exposed roads between climbs
Temperature differential of 15-20C between valley floors and alpine summits
Pre-dawn start means cold first hour before temperatures climb rapidly
Bushfire smoke in bad years — reduced air quality and visibility
UV exposure is intense in Australian summer — sunburn is a genuine risk on a 10+ hour ride

terrain.primary: Paved alpine roads through Victorian High Country
terrain.surface: Well-maintained sealed roads throughout. Australian alpine highways connecting ski resort towns. Wide lanes on main roads, narrower on summit approaches. All tarmac.
features: Mount Hotham (1,861m) — 30 km of sustained climbing, the longest and highest ascent
Tawonga Gap (1,120m) — shorter but punchy climb connecting valleys
Falls Creek (1,780m) — the final summit, climbed on empty legs after 200+ km
Long, fast descents between peaks — Hotham descent into Harrietville is 30+ km

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: A linear epic across the Victorian Alps. The three-peak structure gives the ride a natural narrative: conquer Hotham, survive the valley heat, endure Tawonga, and somehow find the legs for Falls Creek at the end. The terrain alternates between long sustained climbs to cool alpine summits and hot, exposed valley roads where the sun beats down relentlessly. There is almost no flat ground — you are either climbing, descending, or wishing you were descending.
course_description.signature_challenge: The cumulative weight of 235 km and 4,000m. No single climb is the hardest you'll ever ride, but stacking three alpine summits in a single day — with valley heat between them — creates an event where the whole is devastatingly greater than the sum of its parts.
suffering_zones: The Final Ascent to Falls Creek

logistics.airport: Melbourne (MEL) — 4.5 hours drive. No closer commercial airports. Most riders drive up and stay in Falls Creek or nearby Bright/Mount Beauty.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Falls Creek village for proximity to the start/finish. Ski lodges operate in summer at lower rates but availability is limited — book 3-6 months ahead. Bright (1.5 hours from Falls Creek) is the larger town alternative with more options. Mount Beauty (base of the Falls Creek climb) is another option.
logistics.food: Alpine Australian — pub meals, bakeries, and basic supplies in Falls Creek village. More dining options in Bright. Self-catering in a ski lodge is common. Feed stations provide standard cycling nutrition — gels, bananas, sandwiches, electrolytes.

history.origin_story: Created by Bicycle Network to give Australian cyclists a genuine alpine challenge that could stand alongside the European mountain cyclosportives. The route connecting three Victorian alpine ski resorts — Falls Creek, Mount Hotham, and via Tawonga Gap — leverages the best climbing terrain in mainland Australia. The 3 Peaks concept became the defining challenge in Australian road cycling within a few years of launch.
notable_moments: 2012: Inaugural edition establishes the 3 Peaks format
Mount Hotham's 30 km climb becomes recognized as one of Australia's great road climbs
Event grows to 2,000-3,000 riders, becoming Australia's premier climbing sportive
Bushfire years test event resilience and rider toughness
history.reputation: The 3 Peaks. If you've finished it, you belong to a club. Australia's most respected single-day cycling achievement.

biased_opinion.summary: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek is Australia's answer to the great European mountain cyclosportives, and it holds its own. The format is brutally simple: climb three alpine summits, cover 235 km, gain 4,000 meters. The Victorian High Country provides the backdrop — eucalyptus forests, alpine meadows, panoramic ridgeline views — but the real story is the sheer physical and mental challenge of the day. Mount Hotham's 30 km climb is the physical crux. The final ascent to Falls Creek at kilometer 220, on legs that have been working for 10+ hours, is the emotional one. If you finish, you know exactly what you're made of.
strengths: Ultra distance: 235 km with 4,000m of climbing creates a genuine all-day mountain epic
Three-peak structure gives the ride narrative and purpose — each summit is a milestone
Mount Hotham: 30 km of sustained climbing to 1,861m is a world-class climb
The finish: arriving atop Falls Creek on empty legs after 220+ km is cycling at its most raw
weaknesses: Geographic isolation: Falls Creek is 4.5 hours from Melbourne, which is itself remote from most of the world
Valley heat: 30-35C on exposed roads between climbs is punishing and potentially dangerous
Limited international profile: largely unknown outside Australian cycling circles
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Three mountains. 235 kilometers. One day. If you want to know your limits, the Victorian Alps will show you.

final_verdict.one_liner: Australia's definitive mountain challenge. Three alpine summits. 235 km. 4,000 meters of climbing in a single day.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuine ultra-distance mountain cycling challenge outside Europe. Yes if completing Australia's most respected single-day cycling event is on your list. Skip if you can't commit to training for 10-14 hours in the saddle. Skip if the 4.5-hour drive from Melbourne (or long-haul flight from overseas) is a dealbreaker.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want alpine climbing in Australia with less distance: try the individual 7 Peaks rides. If you want ultra-distance cycling in a different format: try the Indian Pacific Wheel Race. If you want a European mountain sportive: try La Marmotte or the Maratona dles Dolomites.

citations.label: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek — Bicycle Network

youtube_data.search_query: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek gran fondo cycling Falls Creek

videos.title: Peaks Challenge 2026  | The Journey Starts NOW
videos.description: Peaks challenge Falls Creek. 235 km. 4500m of climbing. 13 hour cut off time. 1000s make the start, but many don’t finish. Follow Michelle and her athletes as they embark open a journey to Falls Creek in March 2026
videos.transcript:
In March 2026,
six riders, six different stories, one
massive event ahead.
Peaks challenge Falls Creek, 235 km,
4,000 m of climbing, 13-hour cutoff.
Thousands make the start, many don't
finish.
I'm returning to Falls Creek in 2026 and
I'm taking five other athletes along
with me.
For people who don't know me, I am a
cycling coach, but I also enjoy cycling
a lot. I enjoy all forms of cycling from
road, gravel, CX, and of course big
events.
I always said I would never do this
event ever again because I had really
good prep and a really good event back
in 2021. However, if everything goes
well for me this year, first and
foremost is to finish, but secondly, I'd
still like to get close to the 11 hours.
>> The one thing I want to avoid is it to
be really hot, get cramp, be sick, cuz I
don't do too well in the heat.
I'm inspired by all the women at this
stage in life pushing the limits. It's
really difficult being a permenopausal
athlete and a coach and a mom. Those
women who are pushing the boundaries and
proving that just because you're a
little bit older doesn't mean that you
can stop enjoying life and pushing
yourself.
My biggest challenge over the next three
months is actually fitting in the
training around my work commitments. Now
trying to fit all that in around my
current expectations of what training
should look like.
Hotham is monumental but at least it's
broken down to three stage. I think I am
most worried about WTF and the back of
falls
pigs challenge in three words. Brutal,
beautiful, challenging.

videos.title: Can I Go Sub 9 Hours at Peaks Challenge 2025? | My Sub-9 Peaks Plan // Episode 4
videos.description:
RACE DAY! Follow me as I travel from Sydney to Falls Creek for the 2025 Peaks Challenge.

My goal is to finish the 235km 4500m Gran Fondo in less than 9 hours (a whopping 45m faster than last year). Sub9Peaks!

Episode 4 brings all the previous plans together for one make or break day.

🚴‍♂️ Peaks Challenge 2025 (🚨 Spoiler Alert) https://strava.app.link/Mw2viy8nMRb

🏷️ #Cycling #RoadCycling #PeaksChallenge #Sub9Peaks

💨 Get yourself an electric pump. Better than C02 - CYCPLUS AS2 Tiny E-Pump (referral link) - https://www.cycplus.com/?ref=hfrkcicm

🎵 Find all the tracks from all my videos in this handy playlist (Referral link) https://share.epidemicsound.com/8lgfzh/?playlist=xlf2mzt3lywqdnhq9dxdo5lbgejd9v11

👉🏻 Follow me on Instagram and Strava
➡️ https://www.instagram.com/moderatecyclist/
➡️ https://www.strava.com/athletes/bkpaton

00:00 - Trailer
01:37 - Introduction
02:17 - Friday // Travel
03:06 - Saturday // Pre-Race
06:56 - Sunday // Race Day
08:47 - Climb // Tawonga Gap
13:10 - Climb // Mt Hotham
22:26 - Climb // Back of Falls
26:16 - Race // Finish
27:05 - Wrap-up

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race day experience with good production value and specific goal-focused narrative.

videos.title: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek 2024 highlights
videos.description:
On Sunday 10 March, 2000 riders tackled the gruelling 235km loop, battling scorching conditions and unforgiving terrain to etch their stories into the legend of Peaks Challenge. From moments of triumph to unwavering perseverance, this year's event was truly one for the history books.

Secure your spot at Peaks Challenge 2025 with a super early bird ticket before Sunday 31 March to score the best price! Make your mark on Peaks history.

Commit Now: https://bicyclenetwork.com.au/rides-and-events/peaks-challenge/

it takes everything you've got mentally
and physically to conquer this
Beast

videos.title: Chasing a Gran Fondo RECORD … Inside the 225km Peaks Challenge Epic Presented by INSTA360 GO3s
videos.description:
Peaks Challenge is one of the toughest one day cycling events in Australia, this year however I've come to event in some of my best shape for years, chasing a time a can be truly proud of. 

🛒 Get FREE Insta360 Go3 s Accessory link: https://www.insta360.com/sal/go-3s?utm_term=INRVAG9

📺 New Episodes of The Nero Show every week: http://bit.ly/2gMWc1t

👔 Support the show by buying The NERO Skinsuit: https://nero-club.myshopify.com

💻 For advertising opportunities, email us at: info@nero.bike

Our Links:
NERO Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/neroshowcycling/
Chris Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/chrismiller27/
Jesse Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jessecoyle/
Coaching Inquiries for Jesse: https://www.nero.club/coaching

Additional Footage Courtesy of:
@BicycleNetwork 
@ItsMaxMann

Chapters
00:00 intro
00:58 Crazy Descent
02:13 First Climb
03:37 Bottle Gate
08:27 Nutrition Used
10:00 Bike Check 
11:22 Back Of Falls
13:33 Why Sub 7 Matters
14:21 Run For Home

videos.curation_reason: High-quality first-person race recap from 2025 with excellent view count and detailed course description.

quotes.text: The course itself starts with this descent 45 minutes down an Alpine descent then onto toong gap which is basically an FTP test 20 minutes then the Monster uh an hour hour and a half of climbing homeo frying pan.
Tomorrow is what it feels like to ride the stage of the tour to France. This is why this ride is ranked in the top 10 grand fondos in the world.
We're all Lemmings trying to follow him and we just essentially Ride The Climb at threshold - this climb itself in comparison to last year I did 345 Watts basically a minute faster.

key_challenges.name: Tawonga Gap
key_challenges.description: An FTP test climb of just over 20 minutes that's consistently steep. Riders describe it as essentially riding the climb at threshold, especially when strong riders like Mark O'Brien push the pace.

key_challenges.name: The Monster
key_challenges.description: An hour to hour and a half of climbing including Hotham, frying pan sections that riders describe as particularly challenging.

key_challenges.name: Opening descent
key_challenges.description: 45 minute Alpine descent in the dark with 2,500+ riders that's fast flowing but has potholes that can cause riders to lose bottles.

terrain_notes.text: The opening descent is described as fast flowing, allowing riders to bomb down it, but contains potholes that can catch riders off guard.
Tawonga Gap descent is described as a cracker and the best descent of all the descents in the event.

race_day_tips.text: Hydration is critical - one rider lost a bottle on the opening descent and described having only 750ml for a 7-hour ride as a serious problem that just won't fly.
Pack emergency bailout bags for feed stations - one rider sent bags to both dinner point and Angler's Rest with extra carbs, bars, and even a mini can of Coke.
Focus on managing efforts, keeping fuel going in, and having strategy to follow moves and adapt accordingly rather than just completing the event.

additional_quotes.text: There's basically no lines at the bike check. Really should have waited now instead of lining up like a sucker.
Phil just saved my life. Phil just gives me a bottle.

rider_intel.search_text: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek is described as equivalent to a Tour de France stage and ranked as one of the top 10 grand fondos in the world. The 235km route with 4,500m elevation gain starts with a 45-minute Alpine descent in darkness with 2,500+ riders, featuring fast flowing terrain but dangerous potholes. Tawonga Gap is a consistent 20-minute FTP test climb that riders tackle at threshold pace, followed by its cracker descent that's considered the best of the event. The Monster section includes hour-plus climbs through Hotham and frying pan areas. Hydration management is critical as riders face 7+ hour efforts. The event takes everything mentally and physically, with riders describing it as a beast that will test limits. Feed station logistics are important with riders sending emergency bags to Angler's Rest and dinner point containing extra carbs and supplies.
